items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper output encoding (webhooks) leading to Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: This vulnerability, rated CVSS 10.0, is an unauthenticated, zero-interaction flaw in the webhooks component of Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that can lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type leading to Privilege Escalation
items.summary: A critical (CVSS 9.6) file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source allows attackers to plant malicious files on the server, potentially leading to privilege escalation.

items.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, opening the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce, caused by a vulnerable third-party component, can be triggered remotely without authentication, leading to application crashes and storefront disruption.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization flaw in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVSS 7.5) in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the application.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw, named PolyShell, in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, leading to potential remote code execution.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized code, and compromise customer accounts.
